channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Musk bets Tesla's AI future on Intel node that isn't finished yet
item.description: Elon Musk announced Tesla's plans to develop AI chips using Intel's unfinished 14A process during an earnings call, highlighting a significant reliance on unproven technology. This move underscores Tesla's ambition to create its own silicon for AI applications.

item.title: AI bats away ping-pong challenge as rise of the machines continues
item.description: A Sony project has achieved a breakthrough in AI, allowing machines to outperform humans in ping-pong, showcasing advancements in speed and accuracy. This development marks a new milestone in the evolution of AI capabilities in competitive sports.

item.title: Sonos Era 300 vs. Denon Home 400: Why I'm pulling the plug on the more popular speaker
item.description: A comparison between the Sonos Era 300 and Denon Home 400 reveals the latter's higher price may not justify its features. The review provides insights into sound quality and value for money in the smart speaker market.

item.title: The best Amazon Fire TV Stick VPNs of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A guide to the best VPNs for Amazon Fire TV Stick has been published, emphasizing privacy and streaming freedom. The article reviews various VPNs that enhance user experience while streaming content.

item.title: If malware via monitor cables is a matter of national security, this might be the gadget for you
item.description: The UK’s GCHQ has launched a new device designed to prevent cyberattacks via monitor cables, although details remain limited. This gadget aims to bolster national security against emerging cyber threats.

item.title: Google Meet or Google Mute? Even CEOs get borked sometimes
item.description: Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian experienced video conferencing issues during a meeting, highlighting the universal challenges of remote communication. This incident reflects the ongoing struggles many face with digital meetings.

item.title: The best smart TV VPNs of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best smart TV VPNs has been released, focusing on security and streaming capabilities. The article aims to help users choose the right VPN to enhance their smart TV experience.

item.title: Will fusion power get cheap? Don’t count on it.
item.description: A study suggests that while fusion power could provide a clean energy source, it may not become affordable. The research indicates that the costs of developing fusion technology might remain high despite its potential benefits.

item.title: Sharing isn’t caring if it’s an admin password
item.description: An article discusses the pitfalls of sharing admin passwords, emphasizing how simplifying access for developers can lead to significant security risks. It serves as a cautionary tale about cybersecurity practices.

item.title: The best free VPNs of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A list of the best free VPNs has been compiled for users seeking budget-friendly options for online privacy. The article reviews various free services to help users make informed choices.

item.title: Elevating Austria: Google invests in its first data center in the Alps.
item.description: Google has announced its first data center investment in the Austrian Alps, aiming to enhance its cloud services in Europe. This move signifies Google's commitment to expanding its infrastructure in strategically important regions.

item.title: Stale gov.uk pages are feeding AI overviews old data and Brits are believing it
item.description: AI-generated summaries from outdated UK government pages are misleading the public, according to content designers. This issue highlights the challenges of relying on stale data for accurate information dissemination.

item.title: Pass the key, passwords have passed their sell-by date
item.description: The UK's National Cyber Security Centre has endorsed passkeys as the new standard for authentication, advising users to abandon passwords. This shift marks a significant change in cybersecurity practices.

item.title: Microsoft launches ‘vibe working’ in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
item.description: Microsoft is launching a new 'Agent Mode' in its Office apps, enhancing the Copilot experience for users. This feature aims to improve productivity by providing more powerful AI assistance.

item.title: gemini-cli-headless 5.0.2
item.description: The gemini-cli-headless 5.0.2 is a new Python wrapper for Google's Gemini Node.js CLI, designed to be resilient and zero-dependency. This tool aims to simplify interactions with Google's services.

item.title: Pangea Buys an AI ‘Genome’ for Travel Itineraries in Third Acquisition: Exclusive
item.description: Pangea has acquired an AI-driven 'genome' for travel itineraries, aiming to combat the homogenization of travel recommendations. This acquisition is part of Pangea's strategy to enhance personalized travel experiences.

item.title: GitHub CLI now collects pseudoanonymous telemetry
item.description: GitHub CLI has introduced pseudoanonymous telemetry collection, allowing for improved user experience while maintaining privacy. This update aims to enhance the command line interface's functionality.

item.title: MUFG lending head signals further Asia dealmaking
item.description: MUFG's lending head indicated potential for increased deal-making in Asia, particularly targeting non-bank financial companies. This signals a shift in investment strategies in the region.

item.title: Researcher claims Claude Desktop installs “spyware” on macOS
item.description: A security researcher claims that Claude Desktop installs spyware on macOS, raising concerns about user privacy and security. This allegation prompts a closer examination of the software's safety.

item.title: uipath-langchain-client 1.9.7
item.description: The uipath-langchain-client 1.9.7 has been released, offering LangChain-compatible chat models and embeddings for UiPath's LLM services. This update enhances the integration of AI in automation workflows.

item.title: Apple is about to reinvent Siri at WWDC this year — and it might finally feel like ChatGPT
item.description: Apple is set to revamp Siri with a focus on conversational AI and improved integration, aiming to modernize the assistant to compete with ChatGPT. This overhaul is significant as it reflects Apple's commitment to enhancing user experience in AI technology.

item.title: tokenxray 0.3.5
item.description: TokenXray 0.3.5 allows users to analyze AI coding token usage, helping track costs and identify inefficiencies in Claude Code sessions. This tool is important for developers managing expenses in AI projects.

item.title: Morning Minute: Bitcoin Passes $78k as Trump Extends Ceasefire Indefinitely
item.description: Bitcoin has surged past $78,000, while Kevin Warsh faced Senate scrutiny, and Kalshi and Polymarket are entering the perpetual futures market. This uptick in Bitcoin is notable as it reflects growing interest and investment in cryptocurrencies.

item.title: claude-switchboard 1.0.26
item.description: Claude-switchboard 1.0.26 is a multi-account manager for Claude Code CLI profiles, streamlining user experience across different accounts. This update is useful for developers managing multiple projects or clients.

item.title: Why OpenAI’s New ChatGPT Image 2 is a Massive Leap for AI Generation
item.description: OpenAI's ChatGPT Image 2 introduces significant advancements in AI-driven image generation, enhancing detail and reasoning capabilities. This upgrade is crucial as it sets a new standard for image generation technology.

item.title: Show HN: Scan your Airtable base and get a Postgres migration plan
item.description: A new tool audits Airtable bases for migration readiness, providing schema analysis and actionable plans for transitioning to Postgres. This is significant for businesses looking to optimize their data management.

item.title: Humanoid Robot Market Size Worth USD 8.78 Billion by 2035 Driven by AI Advancements and Expanding Industrial and Consumer Applications
item.description: The humanoid robot market is projected to reach USD 8.78 billion by 2035, driven by advancements in AI and expanding applications. This growth indicates a rising interest in humanoid robots across various sectors.

item.title: How to Use Gemini AI for Free in 2026 – Everything You Can Do Without Paying
item.description: A guide on using Gemini AI for free in 2026 details features available without payment, including hidden tools and comparisons with paid tiers. This information is valuable for users seeking cost-effective AI solutions.

item.title: orbit-dashboard 1.0.1
item.description: Orbit Dashboard 1.0.1 provides task analytics and monitoring for Claude Code projects, enhancing project management capabilities. This tool is essential for developers looking to optimize their workflows.

item.title: Tencent, Alibaba in talks to invest in DeepSeek at over $20 billion valuation, The Information reports
item.description: Tencent and Alibaba are reportedly in talks to invest in AI startup DeepSeek, which is valued at over $20 billion. This potential investment underscores the competitive landscape in the AI sector among major tech firms.

item.title: Hands-on with ChatGPT's powerful new image engine - Axios
item.description: OpenAI has upgraded ChatGPT with its new Images 2.0 model, which significantly improves its ability to generate images, including non-Latin text. This enhancement showcases the growing capabilities of AI in visual content creation.

item.title: kagent-openai 0.9.0b8
item.description: The latest release of KAgent includes OpenAI integration with support for A2A server functionality, enhancing its capabilities for developers. This update signifies ongoing advancements in AI integration with various applications.

item.title: SamuRoid – A Raspberry Pi-powered 22-DOF humanoid robot with Multimodal LLMs and ROS support
item.description: SamuRoid is a new humanoid robot powered by Raspberry Pi, featuring 22 degrees of freedom and support for multimodal LLMs and ROS. This robot aims to aid researchers and educators in robotics development.

item.title: cordum-guard 2.10.0
item.description: Cordum has released version 2.10.0 of its Safety Guard, which includes a decorator for LangChain, LlamaIndex, and Python functions. This update aims to enhance safety and functionality in coding.

item.title: Open Channels FM: Scaling WooCommerce Operations with Automation
item.description: A podcast episode discusses how WooCommerce operations can be scaled through automation tools like Zapier, highlighting the role of AI in reducing errors and improving efficiency.

item.title: Trojan Virus from Emulator software
item.description: A user reported a Trojan virus infection after downloading emulator software, prompting discussions on malware removal. This highlights ongoing cybersecurity concerns in software downloads.

item.title: Tesla’s Quiet Moneymaker, Its Battery Business, Suddenly Stalls
item.description: Tesla's energy storage sales, which had been rapidly growing, have unexpectedly stalled this year, raising concerns about the company's battery business. This could impact Tesla's overall financial health.

item.title: Google Ads AI-Qualified Call Conversions
item.description: Google Ads has introduced AI-Qualified Call Conversions, which uses AI to assess the quality of call recordings. This aims to improve lead quality for advertisers.

item.title: Google just fixed one of the biggest Gemini for Home problems, bringing back a feature users say they 'really missed'
item.description: Google has resolved a major issue with Gemini for Home, restoring a feature that allows it to engage in conversations similar to the previous Google Assistant. This update aims to improve user experience.

item.title: SpaceX's IPO could be a real problem for Tesla
item.description: As Tesla prepares to report its earnings, attention is drawn to SpaceX's potential IPO, which could pose challenges for Tesla's market position. Investors are closely monitoring both companies.

item.title: Don’t Blame the Model
item.description: The article discusses the reliability of large language models (LLMs), highlighting how minor input changes can lead to significant output variations. It emphasizes the importance of understanding LLM limitations rather than solely blaming the models themselves.

item.title: Scotland Yard can keep using live facial recognition on people in London, say judges
item.description: Judges have ruled that London's Metropolitan Police can continue using live facial recognition technology, stating it complies with current human rights laws. This decision allows the police to enhance surveillance capabilities despite public concerns.

item.title: Afresh secures $34m to expand AI grocery platform
item.description: Afresh has secured $34 million in funding to expand its AI grocery platform, aiming to enhance partnerships with retailers and develop next-generation AI tools. This investment signifies growing interest in AI applications in the grocery sector.

item.title: Intapp (INTA) Expands Partnership to Integrate Activator Research into Celeste AI Platform
item.description: Intapp has expanded its partnership to integrate Activator Research into its Celeste AI platform, enhancing its offerings for legal and professional services. This move reflects the ongoing trend of AI integration in specialized industries.

item.title: model-council-mcp-server 0.1.2
item.description: This article introduces an MCP server that provides outputs from Grok, Gemini, and DeepSeek for Claude to summarize. It showcases advancements in AI model integration and summarization capabilities.

item.title: perplexity-polza-mcp-server 0.1.1
item.description: The article describes an MCP server designed for Perplexity models via the Polza.ai chat API. It highlights the ongoing development of AI communication tools.

item.title: uipath-langchain-client 1.9.6
item.description: This article discusses the release of LangChain-compatible chat models and embeddings for UiPath's LLM services. It reflects the growing integration of AI in automation processes.

item.title: Bitcoin's Price Without Michael Saylor? Grok Thinks Strategy Responsible For 'Real Upward Pressure And Sentiment Tailwinds'
item.description: AI assistant Grok suggests that Bitcoin's price would have been lower without the significant purchases by Strategy Inc., indicating the influence of strategic investments on market sentiment. This insight highlights the interconnectedness of AI analysis and financial markets.

item.title: SoundHound to buy LivePerson to expand conversational AI
item.description: SoundHound plans to acquire LivePerson to enhance its conversational AI capabilities, merging voice AI technology with digital messaging. This acquisition aims to improve customer engagement for enterprises globally.

item.title: tts-webui.index-tts 2.0.1
item.description: The article discusses IndexTTS2, a breakthrough in emotionally expressive and duration-controlled auto-regressive zero-shot text-to-speech technology. This advancement represents a significant step forward in TTS capabilities.
